free_text: + AKITA UNIV.,JPN.
Photofission of protons from berillium and carbon.
1.3  GeV  electron synchrotron at the
Institute for nuclear study, University of Tokio.
Magnetic   spectrometer   consisred    of
bending  magnet,  trigger scintillation counters and
multiwire proportional chambers.
The   separation   of  protons  from
positrons, pions, and deuterons was made by TOF.
Direct reaction proton counting.
In process of differential cross section  calculating
the corrections for the following effects were done:
- efficiency  of  the  trajectory reconstruction was
about 93 %  at 500 MeV/c and about  90  %  at  800
MeV/c;
- accidental coincidences was about 15 %;
- the  pion and deuteron contamination in the proton
events were estimated at less than 1 %;
- the counting rate without target was less than 0.5
- the  nuclear  absorption corrections at the target
and spectrometer were about 4 %  at 400 MeV/c  and
about 2 % at momentum greater than 600 MeV/c.
Statistical    errors   are   given.   The
quadratic sum of  the  systematic  uncertainties  of
(9.2  -  9.8)  %  was  estimated  for  the following
sources:
- number of tagged photons                   4.6 %;
- number of target nuclei                    2.0 %;
- acceptance of spectrometer                 4.3 %;
- momentum calibration                       0.8 5;
- empty target subtraction                   1.0 %;
- accidental coincidence                   < 0.8 %;
- nuclear absorption                       < 5.0 %;
- storage efficiency                         0.8 %;
- trajectory reconstruction efficiency       3.5 %;
- proton identification                1.0 - 2.8 %.
Momentum spectra  of  protons in the reactions of g +
be -> p + anthing and g + c ->  p  +  anything  were
measured  in the proton energy range from 180 to 420
MeV for berillium and from 340 to 580 MeV for carbon
by  use  of  a  tagged  photon  beam.  the  spectrum
obtained shows two peaks which are interpreted to be
due  to  the protons in the reactions g + "n" -> p +
pi and g + "pn" -> p + n, where "n" and "pn" are the
quasifree nucleons and proton-neutron systems in the
target nuclei.
Data from the Table 1.
Data were   compiled   at   the   USA  BNL  NNDC  by
V.V.Varlamov.
